description_long: This is a vintage wooden railway carriage with a rectangular body, painted in a warm brownish-orange hue. The carriage features a series of evenly spaced rectangular windows along both sides, each window framed with wood and some slightly fogged, suggesting age. The roof is gently curved and painted a muted gray, with four round ventilation domes spaced along its length. The undercarriage is black, with visible metal wheels, axles, and suspension components, and the carriage sits on a pair of metal rails. One side door is open, revealing a glimpse of the interior, and the doors are wooden with inset panels. The ends of the carriage have buffers, couplings, and chains, typical of historical passenger train cars.
description: A vintage wooden railway carriage with a curved gray roof, rectangular windows, and detailed undercarriage, featuring an open side door and classic train fittings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the carriage, with all windows and an open side door visible.
description_view_1: End view showing the flat face of the carriage, buffers, couplings, and the open door protruding to the left.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with all windows visible and no open doors, highlighting the symmetry of the carriage.
description_view_3: Rear end view similar to view 1, showing the flat end, buffers, and couplings, but the open door is less visible.
attribute_door_placement: doors at each end and one side door
